title: Tick 27: log the IDLE hold-pattern observation now that all 31 outcomes are either done blocked or held for human merge
question: Is the orchestrator in a verifiable IDLE state in which every QUEUE row is either done blocked on an operator decision or in_progress awaiting human merge such that there is no atomic step the heartbeat can productively take this tick without operator input?
success_criteria: QUEUE tick log appends a tick-27 line recording the IDLE observation and naming the specific blockers B5 held for human merge C3 blocked on missing apex zone C6 blocked on AI Gateway misfit
PR opensubagents outcomes-mcp 18 the B5 ratchet is confirmed still open with all 5 CI checks green and auto-merge intentionally null per the hold-for-human-merge rule
the heartbeat does NOT call CronDelete or skip the next tick fire because the operator-only stop rule overrides the loop-prompt's stop-on-idle condition
the tick produces a runroc that future operators can read to confirm the heartbeat understood the hold-pattern state rather than silently degrading

capabilities: QUEUE accurately records 25 done 1 held 2 blocked 0 pending unblocked
B5 PR is mergeable with green checks awaiting operator action
operator-only stop rule honored by the heartbeat
IDLE state recorded as a tick artifact not a no-op
